_comment: CHARACTER-MODEL REGISTRY — animated third-person player-character models. DELIBERATELY separate from props.json / fixtures.json: those catalogs feed the collider-baking + measured-bounds physics pipeline, which a player character must NEVER pass through (it would grow a physics collider it shouldn't have). Nothing here is disguisable or collidable — these are pure view models drawn over the networked player capsule. Loaded by js/config.js into cfg.characterModels and consumed only by js/scene.js. Both GLBs are CC0 by Quaternius (via poly.pizza).

hunter._comment: SWAT soldier shown to OTHER players for a REMOTE hunter (the local hunter stays first-person and never renders this). Clips are 'CharacterArmature|<name>' in the GLB — matched by SUFFIX in scene.js, so the prefix here is advisory only.
hunter._yawComment: yawOffsetDeg rotates the model about vertical so its FRONT faces the networked facing yaw. Set to 180 to fix the 'faces backwards' symptom VRmike saw in live 2-player testing (the soldier's native forward is +Z; the game's is -Z). HOT-TUNABLE: if a live browser shows it still off (running sideways etc.), nudge this — no rebuild.
hunter._clipsComment: RIFLE POSE/ANIM POLISH (2026-07-12, VRmike): a hunter always holds the rifle, so remote viewers must always see the RIFLE (gun-up) run/aim, never the arms-at-sides run. ROOT CAUSE of 'rifle points at the ground': the wrist-bone orientation DIFFERS between clips — in the shoot/aim clips (Idle_Gun_Pointing / Gun_Shoot / Idle_Gun_Shoot / Run_Shoot) the rig poses the wrist so a rifle attached at weapon.rotationDeg=0 points nearly straight DOWN, and the old 'Idle_Gun' idle poses it a DIFFERENT way (barrel backward), so no single grip rotation fixed both. FIX (derived with matrix math from the actual rig pose — tools/_solve_rifle.mjs): (1) idle now uses 'Idle_Gun_Pointing', a real AIM-IDLE that holds the gun raised and pointing forward AND shares the shoot clips' wrist orientation, so the hunter keeps the gun up (never arms-at-side) when standing still; (2) all movement maps to 'Run_Shoot' (the gun-up run, same wrist family); (3) weapon.rotationDeg below is solved once so the muzzle points forward and the gun stays upright across EVERY shoot/aim clip. There is no gun-up strafe/backpedal clip in this GLB, so strafing uses the forward run cycle (legs) while the rifle stays UP + forward. check-hunter-model.mjs asserts every clip here resolves in the GLB AND is a gun/aim clip. HOT-TUNABLE (no rebuild) — confirm the pose live and nudge rotationDeg if the grip roll/facing looks off.

anim._comment: moveThreshold (u/s): below this the hunter plays idle. refSpeed (u/s): the speed at which run clips play at timeScale 1 — matches rules.moveSpeed (6). timeScale is clamped to [minTimeScale,maxTimeScale]. crossfadeSeconds: blend time between clips (no popping).

weapon._comment: Assault rifle parented to the right-wrist bone. worldLength is the rifle's longest-axis size in METRES: scene.js measures the GLB's native size and the wrist bone's world scale (which carries the armature's baked 100x and the rig's fit scale) and sizes the weapon to this length — so the rifle stays correct whatever the body sizing does (the old bare `scale` blew up/shrank when the rig scale changed). position (bone-local units), rotationDeg (bone-local degrees, Euler XYZ) and scale (a MULTIPLIER on worldLength) are HOT-TUNABLE grip nudges — adjust here (no rebuild) if the grip looks off in a live browser. attachBone is matched TOLERANTLY (GLTFLoader loads the GLB's 'Wrist.R' as 'WristR'); Wrist.R / Wrist.L both exist.
weapon._rotationComment: 180 FLIP (2026-07, VRmike): the rifle pointed BACKWARDS in remote/third-person view (screenshot in the request) — the previous solve ASSUMED the barrel was the GLB's -X end and pointed that forward, but VRmike's live view proved the muzzle is actually the +X end, so the gun fired behind the hunter. FIX: use tools/_solve_rifle.mjs's [muzzle+X, up+Y] variant — the SAME rig-derived solve, just with the muzzle axis corrected. It maps the barrel(+X) to the character's forward(-Z) and gun-up(+Y) to world-up, verified numerically against the ACTUAL Wrist.R pose across Idle_Gun_Pointing / Run_Shoot / Gun_Shoot / Idle_Gun_Shoot (barrel=(0,0,-1) forward, up=(0,1,0) in all four — re-run the tool to confirm). This is exactly the requested 180 turn (barrel reversed, gun still upright) relative to the old value. Headless RENDER isn't possible in the build sandbox, so eyeball it live post-deploy; if it still looks off, flip a sign / add 180 to a component here (HOT-TUNABLE, no rebuild).
weapon._forwardOffsetComment: HELD-ITEM DOWN + FORWARD OFFSET (2026-07-19 #188/#190; REDONE 2026-07-20, VRmike). Two nudges applied to ALL THREE held meshes (rifle/grenade/finder share the wrist grip anchor): forward = facing (group -Z), down = model-vertical (group -Y), in world metres, converted to the wrist bone's LOCAL frame (shared/hunter-sizing.js::heldItemBoneOffset) so they ride the arm as it tilts with look pitch (see the `pitch` block) and stay correct as the hunter turns. THESE NUMBERS ARE ALREADY CORRECT — #188/#190 didn't show in-game NOT because the values were wrong but because scene.js computed the bone-local vector in the BIND (A-)pose (above the AnimationMixer); the item renders in the AIM pose where the wrist is rotated ~90°, so the 'down' tipped to UP and never landed. The 2026-07-20 redo POSES the rig into the idle-aim clip BEFORE anchoring the item, so forward+down land as forward+down live (verified in tools/check-held-item-offset.mjs: forward 0.22 m, down 0.17 m, on the hand side, stable across facings). downOffset 0.17 sits in the requested 0.15-0.20 m band. HOT-TUNABLE: raise downOffset if it still floats high, lower it if it sinks; tweak forwardOffset for trail/overshoot; 0 disables either axis. Do NOT re-add offset magnitude to 'compensate' without checking the pose-frame — that's what created the two-build loop.

pitch._comment: REMOTE LOOK-PITCH RIG (2026-07-19, VRmike): remote hunter models always aimed dead-horizontal. scene.js tilts the HEAD and the right UPPER-ARM (which carries the wrist + held item) to match the player's networked look pitch (snapshot `pitch`), so a hunter looking up/down reads correctly to props and the held weapon rides along. Purely visual on remote models — no gameplay/hitbox change. The rotation axis is derived per bone from its world orientation (the character's left-right axis), so it's rig-correct without hand-solving Euler angles. CLAMPED so extreme pitch can't fold the model. HOT-TUNABLE (no rebuild): flip `sign` if up/down is inverted live, scale headFactor/armFactor for how much each bone follows, tighten maxUpDeg/maxDownDeg if the arm/head overrotates, raise `smooth` (0..1 per frame) for snappier tracking.
